text.title: vr-design-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vr-design-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Smooth controller movement as default for "natural feel".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Causes motion sickness in novices; ships high churn and refund rates. Force teleport-first even if it feels outdated.

failure-mode.description: Locomotion comfort is assumed universal ("teleport works for everyone").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Teleport comfort varies wildly by individual; some users prefer smooth movement. Individual playtesting is mandatory.

failure-mode.description: Floating UI panels assumed to follow ergonomics like real-world objects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wrist fatigue rises after ~8 min of pinch input. Sim physics differ from human ergonomics; limit pinch-intensive tasks to <5 min or offer voice/controller alternative.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Meta, SteamVR, and visionOS have different APIs and visual markers. Specify SDK version in prompt and test on each platform.

failure-mode.description: Hand-tracking jitter ignored; assumes precise selection under low light.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hand-tracking fails under low light or outdoor bright sunlight. Always provide controller fallback.

failure-mode.description: Eye-tracking gaze-and-confirm treated as portable across platforms.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: visionOS supports eye-tracking natively; Quest 3 standard doesn't. Pin device.

failure-mode.description: Comfort vignette parameters applied as agent defaults without playtest tuning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vignette size, fade-in time, and persistence vary by user comfort tolerance. Defaults are conservative-to-broken.
